reasoning: The Federal Reserve just met on May 6-7, 2025, and decided to keep interest rates unchanged at 4.25%-4.50%. The statement highlighted rising economic uncertainty, and Powell indicated the next step is "not at all clear." The CME FedWatch tool, according to recent articles (early May), implies only a 1-in-3 chance of ANY rate cut in June, with 0 chance of a 50 bps cut priced in. Given that a 50 bps cut is usually reserved for economic emergencies, and the Fed is actively holding rates steady amid inflation risks from tariffs, a 50 bps cut in the very next meeting in June is extremely improbable. Polymarket was at 4% on May 1st, and that was prior to the May 7th meeting where the Fed confirmed their hold stance and highlighted inflation/unemployment dual risks without signaling panic. I estimate the probability of a 50 bps cut at 2%.
